description_long: This object is a Roman-style military standard, featuring a long black pole topped with a pointed spearhead. Suspended from a horizontal crossbar is a deep red rectangular banner adorned with a golden eagle and the letters 'SPQR', flanked by decorative tassels and finials. The pole appears to be made of metal, with gold accents at the ends and joints, and the banner is likely made of fabric. The overall design is ceremonial and symbolic, evoking the grandeur of ancient Roman legions. The banner is rigidly attached to the pole, and the spearhead at the top adds a sense of authority and tradition. The object is visually striking, with bold colors and intricate detailing on the banner and pole.
description: A ceremonial Roman-style standard with a red and gold banner, black pole, and spearhead top, featuring 'SPQR' and an eagle emblem. Decorative tassels and gold accents enhance its historic and authoritative appearance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full red banner with gold eagle and 'SPQR', spearhead top, and decorative tassels.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the pole and spearhead, with the banner barely visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the banner and pole, with the spearhead and gold accents visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile of the pole and spearhead, with minimal banner visibility.
attribute_graphic_design: The banner features a gold eagle with outstretched wings above the letters 'SPQR' on a deep red background, bordered by a gold pattern. Decorative tassels and finials accent the sides and ends of the crossbar.
